critique: The job description clearly outlines technical responsibilities such as "Own end-to-end LLM product surfaces" and "Run rigorous evaluations and experiments across prompts, retrieval, and agents." It also specifies a strong technical foundation in "Python and NLP fundamentals" and provides a competitive salary range, indicating a genuine AI engineering role.

The job description clearly outlines an AI/ML engineering role focused on "Build knowledge graph from web data," indicating substantive technical work. Furthermore, the company shows signs of legitimacy with "strong product-market fit and early revenue" and having "Raised $38.5MM from top-tier VCs," which points to a real, funded venture.

The job description explicitly details a "Research engineer position focused on building AI to simulate the world" and mentions working with "models and infrastructure for creative tools." This clearly indicates a genuine AI engineering role with tangible technical content, aligning with a real product development environment.

The job description clearly outlines an engineering role focused on "Build[ing] AI-powered medical imaging solutions using deep learning across MRI, PET, and CT modalities." It details tangible outcomes like "Solutions deployed on 1,000+ scanners globally," indicating a real product and genuine technical work rather than vague business development or partnership language.

The role description clearly outlines a real engineering position focused on building a "Rust-based SDK for logging, querying, visualizing multimodal data" for "physical AI including robotics and autonomous systems." Despite the lack of compensation details, the technical content of the role is robust and specific, indicating a genuine engineering opportunity.

The `leadlens` classifier indicates "no comp" as a red flag, which aligns with the "equity-only with no salary" pattern common in ai_washed postings. Despite the clear technical description of building "modular probabilistic models for finance and scientific research," the absence of any compensation signal raises concerns about the nature of the engagement.

The role clearly describes an "Integration Engineer" with concrete engineering responsibilities like "Golang backend, custom database engine, server-side HTML rendering." While it mentions "Uses AI extensively," this seems to describe AI as a tool or method within a genuine technical role, rather than using AI vocabulary to disguise a non-engineering position. The description details a specific technical stack and clear project ownership.

The job description details a legitimate infrastructure engineering role focused on "autoscaling systems for cloud database and distributed caching layer on S3," requiring "Go, Kubernetes, distributed systems expertise." It does not exhibit any of the defined "ai_washed" patterns such as partnership opportunities, equity-only compensation with no salary, vague descriptions, or misrepresentation of an AI role.

The job description clearly outlines a technical full-stack engineering role focused on "AI-driven workflow automation for healthcare clinics," utilizing "Next.js and Django" and "LLM APIs extensively." It details specific engineering tasks and responsibilities related to "HIPAA-aware security" and backend systems. While the `leadlens` classifier flagged "no comp," the job description does not explicitly state "no salary for first N months" or an "equity-only" compensation model, which are key indicators of an ai_washed posting under the given criteria.

This job description clearly details a technical AI engineering role, focusing on concrete tasks like "raw FMCW/MIMO signal processing to deployed embedded models (ONNX, TensorRT, edge TPUs)" and "real hardware with real customers." The presence of specific technical content and applications indicates a genuine engineering position, not an "ai_washed" marketing or partnership scheme.

The job description clearly outlines a technical engineering role, focusing on specific AI/LLM frameworks like "LangChain, LlamaIndex, CrewAI, LangGraph" and emphasizing "End-to-end deployment of working AI agents in production - not a demo." This indicates a legitimate AI engineering position with concrete technical responsibilities, not a sales or marketing role disguised with AI terminology.

The job description explicitly details real AI engineering tasks such as "Design and implement Agentic AI workflows using LangChain and LangGraph" and "Build LLM-powered APIs with Python." It also lists concrete technical requirements including "Python, LangChain, LangGraph, REST/GraphQL API design." This clearly indicates a legitimate engineering role with substantive technical content.

This posting describes an "Unpaid internship for 3 months" with a "Performance-based" stipend for a 40-hour work week. While the work itself involves real AI agent development, the compensation structure indicates a highly exploitative model rather than a legitimate engineering role, fitting the pattern of roles with no fixed salary or highly uncertain pay.

The posting clearly outlines a genuine AI engineering role, specifying tasks like "ship a hybrid RAG pipeline with reranking + Ragas evals" and detailing a relevant technical stack including "Python, LangChain, pgvector, Cohere rerank, Modal deploy, Langfuse tracing." This level of technical specificity directly contradicts the patterns associated with "ai_washed" roles, which typically lack real engineering content despite using AI buzzwords.

The job description outlines a clear, project-based engineering task to "build a custom GPT/Claude integration for our CRM" with "Deliverables: working Python script, documentation, 30-day support." While it is a "Fixed-price $2,500 USD" and "No long-term commitment," this is consistent with a legitimate freelance contract and doesn't exhibit typical 'ai_washed' patterns like partnership opportunities or vague equity-only compensation.

The posting clearly states 'Not a job - it's a partnership opportunity' and 'No salary - revenue share,' which are direct indicators of an ai_washed pattern. Furthermore, while the title mentions 'AI Co-Founder,' the required expertise is for 'Digital Marketing generalist, Web Dev - Graphic/video & LeadGen' with AI applied to these areas, rather than core AI engineering.

This posting exhibits several classic 'ai_washed' patterns. Specifically, it offers an 'Equity-only role 1-3%' with 'No salary for first 6 months,' which is a significant red flag. Furthermore, the description is 'Vague on what we're actually building - will explain on a call' and relies on 'Telegram-first communication' and 'viral X posts as proof of traction,' all indicative of a non-standard or potentially misleading opportunity rather than a genuine job.

The job description explicitly states "CTC INR 12-18 LPA fixed + ESOPs" and details engineering tasks such as "Work on India's foundational AI model stack" and "Python, PyTorch fundamentals, exposure to one of (training/fine-tuning, inference optimization, evals)". These elements confirm a genuine engineering role with a clear salary, rather than a partnership or an AI-washed position.

This job posting clearly describes a substantive engineering role focused on "Train and fine-tune foundation models for Indic languages" using specific technologies like "PyTorch + distributed training (FSDP, DeepSpeed)". It explicitly states, "Not application engineering - this is foundational ML/training infrastructure," leaving no ambiguity about the technical depth required.

This posting is ai_washed because the title 'AI Customer Success Manager' misleadingly uses 'AI' while the description explicitly states the 'role involves zero AI engineering' and 'No coding required'. The responsibilities are purely customer-facing, focusing on adoption and configuration, not technical AI development.
